catalysts.catalyst_details: Broadcom maintains an 18-month+ lead over internal customer COT (customer-owned-tooling) programs, coupled with increasing chip/packaging complexity and a rapid cadence of new product introductions, accelerating its AI ASIC business outlook with Google.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: With Broadcom having an 18 month+ lead over internal customer COT programs (customer-owned-tooling – where the customer does 90% of the chip design) and increasing chip/packaging complexity and cadence of new product introductions, the outlook for Broadcom’s AI ASIC programs/business, especially with its largest AI customer, Google (covered by JPM analyst Doug Anmuth), is accelerating - both FY26 and FY27.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Google is targeting to deploy 6-7M TPU processors in CY27 (up from a prior view of 5M units), with 95%+ of these units, including those for external customer AI workloads, powered by Broadcom’s next-gen 3nm TPU ASIC (Sunfish).
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Google themselves continues to have increasing success at supporting external customer AI workloads (Anthropic, OpenAI, Apple, etc) with TPU and our very recent primary research efforts lead us to believe that Google is now targeting to deploy 6-7M TPU processors in CY27 (versus our prior view of 5M units and 3.5M units this year) and more importantly, a majority of these shipments will be to support external customers AI workloads and these engagements will be 100% powered by Broadcom’s/Google’s next-gen 3nm TPU ASIC, code-named Sunfish (or what we have been calling the V7e “hellcat” program). The remaining minority of CY27 TPUs (internal Google usage) will also primarily be powered by Broadcom’s Sunfish 3nm TPU ASIC as well – bottom line, 95%+ of the 6-7M Google TPU units in CY27 will be powered by Broadcom silicon.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Google’s internal COT program, Zebrafish, has experienced numerous delays, with first silicon now expected by the end of Q1 2026 or beginning of Q2 2026, and uncertainty regarding its specifications. This extends Broadcom’s 18-month+ lead.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe that Google’s internal (COT) program, called Zebrafish (3nm, smaller chip, inference-only, 2x less complex versus Broadcom’s TPU), after numerous program delays, will only have first silicon in-hand by end of this quarter or the beginning of next quarter – implying that Broadcom’s Sunfish chip (which Google evaluated/qualified at the BEGINNING of CY25) will have an 18mos+ lead over Google’s COT efforts. Lots of noise on Google COT program (Google 90% chip design/Mediatek 10% contribution) for 3nm Zebrafish TPU - but this program has been delayed multiple times - first silicon now expected end of this quarter or beginning of next quarter (and not clear whether or not this chip will even meet specifications) - which means Broadcom 3nm Sunfish program (successor to Ironwood and fully qualified at Google in 1H of last year) is now 18 months ahead…Not surprising that 95%+ of the forecasted 6-7M TPU shipments in CY27 will continue to be powered by Broadcom silicon.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Broadcom has secured multi-billions of dollars in Purchase Orders (POs) for its AI ASIC ramp, including current-gen (Ironwood) and next-gen (Sunfish) TPU programs, with the ramp commencing in 2H 2026 and extending into 2027. The AI backlog has continued to grow since the December earnings call.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In fact, we believe Broadcom already has multi-billions of dollars in POs for this ramp that commences in the 2H of this year. Broadcom continues to see upside to near/mid-term orders on current-gen TPU program (3nm Ironwood - “Hammer” ) and has secured multi-billions of dollars in POs for next-gen TPU ramp (3nm “Sunfish” or “Hellcat” program) for 2H26 ramp and into 2027. Since the December earnings call, we believe Broadcom’s AI backlog has continued to grow (by billions of dollars) - both for shipments this year (3nm Google Ironwood TPU and next-gen 3nm Sunfish TPU which ramps in 2H) and layering on strong backlog for next year as well.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Increasing lead times for critical AI components (wafers, CoWos, HBM, advanced substrates) create a barrier for unqualified competitors. Broadcom's Sunfish TPU was qualified in 1H 2025, allowing it to secure necessary supply through CY27, while Google's delayed COT program will struggle to secure meaningful volumes.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Additionally, given the extremely tight supply-chain environment (advanced substrates/HBM memory) we believe it will be difficult for Google’s COT program to secure much volume for its program assuming it starts its initial ramp in 2H27. Given increasing leadtimes for wafers, CoWos, HBM, advanced substrates - if your AI TPU/XPU is not qualified by now, it will be difficult to secure enough supply to ship meaningful volumes in CY27. Broadcom’s next-gen Google 3nm TPU (Sunfish) has been qualified since 1H of last year, and Google/Google’s customers have evaluated the Sunfish TPU and orders have been placed for this year as well as next year (Broadcom Sunfish TPU will power 100% of external customer TPU demand requirements in CY27) and this has provided Broadcom with the confidence to secure wafers/CoWos/HBM/advanced substrates through CY27. On the flip side, even if Google’s COT TPU program (Zebrafish) were to be qualified (9-12mos) after first silicon comes out in 2-3 months, it will be very difficult for this program to secure much supply to ship meaningful volumes in CY27.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Broadcom has won and commenced the design of Google’s next-gen 2nm TPU ASIC, which is targeted for volume production in CY28, further solidifying its long-term partnership and competitive lead.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Furthermore, as we have previously written (see note here), Broadcom has won and already commenced design of Google’s next-gen TPU ASIC (2nm) targeted for volume production in CY28. Also, by the 2H of 27, Broadcom will already be starting to ramp its next-next gen 2nm TPU program with Google….and continuing to maintain its 18 month+ lead over any COT programs…..18 months is 1.5 chip generations, an almost insurmountable competitive lead for anyone competing with Broadcom.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Meta’s 3nm programs (Athena and Iris) are on track for their respective ramps this year, and the 2nm program (Olympus) is on track for a CY27 ramp, indicating diversified revenue streams for Broadcom beyond Google.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Meta 3nm programs (Athena and Iris programs) are still on track for their respective ramps this year and 2nm program (Olympus) is on track for CY27 ramp.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The market continues to underestimate Broadcom’s significant dominance, 18-month+ lead, chip/advanced packaging design leadership, aggressive cadence of new designs, IP portfolio, and strong track record of execution.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The market continues to underestimate Broadcom’s significant dominance/lead (18mos+), chip/advanced packaging design leadership, aggressive cadence of new designs, IP portfolio, and track record of execution (Broadcom has helped Google bring to market 14 of its most advanced chip designs to market over the past 12 years). The market continues to underestimate Broadcom’s significant dominance/lead (18mos+), chip/package design leadership, aggressive cadence of new designs, IP portfolio, and track record of execution (Broadcom has helped Google bring to market 14 its most advanced chip design to market over the past 12 years).

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Stronger Google TPU Outlook for FY26 and FY27.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Google TPU Outlook Getting Stronger FY26 And FY27

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Broadcom's 18-month lead over customer-owned-tooling (COT) initiatives implies shrinking/negligible competition.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: 18 Month Lead Implies Shrinking/Negligible Volumes For COT Initiatives

Broadcom’s next-gen Google 3nm TPU (Sunfish) has been qualified since 1H of last year, and Google/Google’s customers have evaluated the Sunfish TPU and orders have been placed for this year as well as next year (Broadcom Sunfish TPU will power 100% of external customer TPU demand requirements in CY27) and this has provided Broadcom with the confidence to secure wafers/CoWos/HBM/advanced substrates through CY27. bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Broadcom's long-standing leadership in the ASIC market (30+ years), comprehensive know-how, broad IP portfolio, and execution track record provide a significant competitive advantage in AI.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Broadcom has led the ASIC market for 30+ years…it’s not just about SERDES technology…it’s compute/networking/memory/storage architecture know-how, broad IP portfolio, advanced chip/packaging design know-how…and execution…and should continue to be a significant competitive advantage in maintaining its dominance in AI.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Broadcom is a leader in multiple high-growth end markets including wireless, data center networking, AI/deep learning ASICs, storage, and infrastructure silicon/hardware/software.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We see Broadcom as a leader in wireless, data center networking, AI/deep learning ASICs, storage, and infrastructure silicon, hardware, software with broad-based exposure to positive trends in these end markets.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Broadcom is a technology infrastructure powerhouse with unmatched scale and technology capabilities, securing leadership positions in diverse end markets.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Broadcom is a technology infrastructure powerhouse with unmatched scale and technology capabilities in the industry, securing its leadership positions in a diverse set of end markets.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Broadcom exhibits best-in-class operating/free cash flow margins, a diversified business, and AI ASIC/networking leadership, supporting its valuation.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our Dec-26 PT assumes A VGO trades at a 32x multiple (broadly in-line with AI peers trading at 30-35x given its best-in-class operating/free cash flow margins, diversified business, AI ASIC/networking leadership) applied to our CY26E exit run-rate earnings power of ~$15.00 per share.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Strong financial estimates project significant year-over-year growth in revenue (57.5%) and adjusted EPS (60.2%) for FY26.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Revenue 63,887 100,592 Adj. EPS 6.82 10.92 Revenue Growth Y/Y (%) 23.9% 57.5% Adj. EPS growth 40.3% 60.2%

thesis_analysis.central_thesis: Broadcom is poised for significant growth and continued market dominance in AI ASIC programs, particularly with Google, due to its substantial technological lead, strong execution, and strategic positioning in a high-demand market, which the market currently underestimates.

sub_claims.claim: Broadcom holds an insurmountable competitive lead in AI ASIC development, especially with Google.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom has an 18 month+ lead over internal customer COT programs.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. This is a specific, quantifiable timeframe provided by the analyst, indicating a significant head start in technology development.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom’s Sunfish chip was evaluated/qualified by Google at the beginning of CY25, giving it an 18mos+ lead over Google’s internal COT efforts (Zebrafish).
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. Specific dates and qualification status from Google provide strong, verifiable evidence of Broadcom's lead.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom has won and already commenced design of Google’s next-gen TPU ASIC (2nm) targeted for volume production in CY28.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. This confirms a future design win and continued partnership with Google, extending Broadcom's dominance into the next generation.

evidence_list.content: 18 months is 1.5 chip generations, an almost insurmountable competitive lead for anyone competing with Broadcom.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium-High. While 'almost insurmountable' is subjective, it's an expert opinion based on the significant technical lead in a fast-evolving industry, lending weight to the claim of strong competitive advantage.

evidence_list.content: Google’s internal (COT) program, called Zebrafish (3nm, smaller chip, inference-only, 2x less complex versus Broadcom’s TPU), has experienced numerous program delays, with first silicon only expected by end of this quarter or beginning of next quarter.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. Specific details about Google's internal program and its confirmed delays provide a direct, objective contrast to Broadcom's advanced status.

evidence_list.content: It is not clear whether or not Google's Zebrafish chip will even meet specifications.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium. This is an analyst's doubt or speculation, not a confirmed fact. However, it's based on observed delays and technical complexity, adding to the perceived weakness of the competitor.

sub_claims.claim: Broadcom is the primary beneficiary of Google's expanding AI TPU deployments, especially for external customers.

evidence_list.content: Google is now targeting to deploy 6-7M TPU processors in CY27, an increase from the prior view of 5M units and 3.5M units this year.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. This is a quantifiable increase in Google's deployment targets, based on J.P. Morgan's 'very recent primary research efforts', indicating growing demand.

evidence_list.content: A majority of Google's CY27 TPU shipments will support external customer AI workloads (Anthropic, OpenAI, Apple, etc.), and these engagements will be 100% powered by Broadcom’s/Google’s next-gen 3nm TPU ASIC, code-named Sunfish.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. This provides a clear, quantifiable statement of Broadcom's exclusive role in a significant and growing segment of Google's TPU business.

evidence_list.content: 95%+ of the 6-7M Google TPU units in CY27 will be powered by Broadcom silicon.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. This is a strong, quantifiable statement of Broadcom's overwhelming market share within Google's TPU supply for CY27.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom already has multi-billions of dollars in Purchase Orders (POs) for the Sunfish ramp that commences in the 2H of this year.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. The existence of multi-billion dollar POs provides concrete evidence of current and near-term revenue visibility and strong demand.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom continues to see upside to near/mid-term orders on current-gen TPU program (3nm Ironwood) and has secured multi-billions of dollars in POs for next-gen TPU ramp (3nm Sunfish) for 2H26 and into 2027.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. This details ongoing and future revenue streams from both current and next-generation products, reinforcing sustained demand.

evidence_list.content: Since the December earnings call, Broadcom’s AI backlog has continued to grow by billions of dollars for shipments this year and next year.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. Quantifiable growth in backlog indicates robust and increasing demand for Broadcom's AI products.

sub_claims.claim: Broadcom possesses deep technological expertise, a robust IP portfolio, and a proven execution track record that solidifies its market leadership.

evidence_list.content: The market continues to underestimate Broadcom’s significant dominance/lead (18mos+), chip/advanced packaging design leadership, aggressive cadence of new designs, IP portfolio, and track record of execution.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium-High. While 'underestimate' is subjective, it's an expert assessment of market perception, highlighting Broadcom's strong underlying capabilities as perceived by the analyst.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom has helped Google bring to market 14 of its most advanced chip designs over the past 12 years.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. This specific historical data point demonstrates a long-standing, successful partnership and Broadcom's consistent ability to execute complex designs.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom has led the ASIC market for 30+ years.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. This historical fact establishes Broadcom's long-term expertise and sustained leadership in the ASIC market.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom's compute/networking/memory/storage architecture know-how, broad IP portfolio, advanced chip/packaging design know-how, and execution should continue to be a significant competitive advantage in maintaining its dominance in AI.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium-High. This is an expert projection based on Broadcom's established core competencies, providing a strong rationale for future competitive advantage.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom is a leader in wireless, data center networking, AI/deep learning ASICs, storage, and infrastructure silicon, hardware, software with broad-based exposure to positive trends in these end markets.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium-High. 'Leader' is a subjective assessment, but it's an expert opinion from J.P. Morgan, indicating strong market positioning across multiple growth areas.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom is a technology infrastructure powerhouse with unmatched scale and technology capabilities in the industry, securing its leadership positions in a diverse set of end markets.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium-High. 'Powerhouse' and 'unmatched scale' are subjective, but reflect the analyst's strong conviction in Broadcom's robust market position and capabilities.

sub_claims.claim: Broadcom's financial outlook is strong, supported by diversified growth drivers and superior margins.

evidence_list.content: Meta's 3nm programs (Athena and Iris) are on track for ramps this year, and its 2nm program (Olympus) is on track for a CY27 ramp.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. This indicates successful design wins and future revenue streams from a major customer beyond Google, demonstrating diversification and continued growth.

evidence_list.content: Projected revenue growth from $63,887 million in FY25A to $100,592 million in FY26E.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. These are specific financial estimates from J.P. Morgan, indicating substantial top-line growth.

evidence_list.content: Projected adjusted EPS growth from $6.82 in FY25A to $10.92 in FY26E.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. These are specific financial estimates from J.P. Morgan, indicating strong earnings growth.

evidence_list.content: Year-over-year revenue growth projected at 57.5% for FY26E.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. This is a specific, high growth rate estimate from J.P. Morgan, reinforcing the strong financial outlook.

evidence_list.content: Year-over-year adjusted EPS growth projected at 60.2% for FY26E.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High. This is a specific, high growth rate estimate from J.P. Morgan, reinforcing the strong financial outlook.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom has best-in-class operating/free cash flow margins, a diversified business, and AI ASIC/networking leadership.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Medium-High. 'Best-in-class' is subjective, but it's an expert opinion used to justify the valuation, implying strong financial health and operational efficiency.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Unexpected negative shocks to the global economy or regional end markets could lead to changes in demand for Broadcom's products.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: If the global economy or any regional end market suffers unexpected negative shocks that lead to changes in demand for Broadcom’s products, we may reassess our estimates and our Overweight rating.
